title: Moment Russian drone hits apartment block in Romania
content: Video captured the moment a Russian Geran-2 drone crashed into an apartment building in eastern Romania near the Ukrainian border. The impact sparked a fire and injured two people, according to Romanian authorities.

title: EU leaders denounce Russia's 'reckless' escalation after drone hits Romania
content: Ursula von der Leyen, António Costa and Kaja Kallas have condemened Russia for violating Romania's airspace after a drone hit the city of Galați.
snippet: Ursula von der Leyen, António Costa and Kaja Kallas have condemened Russia for violating Romania's airspace after a drone hit the city of Galați.

content: Online child exploitation is increasing across Europe, alongside AI-generated abuse content. Which countries are emerging as hotspots for abuse material?

title: 'Romanian drone incursion is no mistake, it's a provocation,' Polish official tells Euronews
content: Exclusive: Poland’s Undersecretary of State for Trade Michał Baranowski condemned Russia's drone incursion in Romania early Friday morning, which comes hot on the heels to similar incidents in the Baltics and Poland.

title: Podcast | An unusual heatwave with a sinister twist
content: From blistering heatwaves sweeping Europe to rising security tensions along the Baltic border, we dive into the issues keeping officials in Brussels awake this week.
snippet: From blistering heatwaves sweeping Europe to rising security tensions along the Baltic border, we dive into the issues keeping officials in Brussels awake this week.

title: French parliament inches towards symbolic repeal of 'Code noir' slavery legislation
content: France abolished enslaving humans more than 170 years ago and in 2001 recognised slavery and the slave trade as "crimes against humanity."
snippet: France abolished enslaving humans more than 170 years ago and in 2001 recognised slavery and the slave trade as "crimes against humanity."

title: Top-ranked Jannik Sinner crashes out of French Open after apparent struggle with heat
content: Sinner received medical attention and left the court and minerals were added to his drink when he returned but he wasn't able to continue.
snippet: Sinner received medical attention and left the court and minerals were added to his drink when he returned but he wasn't able to continue.

title: Brussels faces backlash over carbon trading system as production costs soar
content: Several EU countries argue that planned cuts to free emissions allowances under the EU Emissions Trading System are too severe and do not reflect the technological limits faced by sectors such as steel, cement, chemicals and aluminium.

title: EU seeks to boost Europe’s chip demand in tech sovereignty bid
content: The EU's revamped Chips Act shifts focus from supply to demand to boost European chip demand, especially for AI. The draft law covers demand aggregation and crisis tools, as part of a broader tech sovereignty push also covering cloud services and open source.

title: Italian police seize more than €200m in late mafia boss Matteo Messina Denaro's assets
content: Assets were being seized in Andorra, the Cayman Islands, Gibraltar, Lebanon, Luxembourg, Monaco, Spain and Switzerland, as well as Italy, police said.
snippet: Assets were being seized in Andorra, the Cayman Islands, Gibraltar, Lebanon, Luxembourg, Monaco, Spain and Switzerland, as well as Italy, police said.

title: Sarkozy insists on innocence in last day of appeal trial in Libya case

title: Canada chooses Sweden's Saab in defence deal over US rivals, PM Mark Carney says
content: Carney says Canada's historically close relationship with the United States has been permanently altered and Ottawa needs to diversify its economic and security relationships.

title: Hungary's Magyar heads to Brussels to reset EU ties and unlock frozen funds

title: Europe Today: Is the EU about to take on a tougher economic stance towards China?
content: EU Commission chief von der Leyen is pulling her team together this morning for urgent talks on whether the EU should take on a tougher economic stance towards China. The closed-door discussion is seen as a first step towards potentially stricter trade measures. Mared Gwyn reports on the story.

title: Germany and Netherlands to set up NATO command centre in Baltics, Berlin says
content: The joint German-Dutch command centre known as 1GNC will "assume a command role on NATO's eastern flank, specifically in the region Estonia and Latvia" in coming months.

title: Hungarian lawmakers vote to reverse exit from ICC initiated by Orbán

content: The appeal sent to the European Commission cites environmental concerns and security risks if the EU decides to lift the ban on oil and gas drilling in the Arctic. title: ICC trial for ex-Philippine President Rodrigo Duterte to start in November, judge says
content: The Philippines left the ICC in 2019, on Duterte's instructions, but the court has ruled that it still has jurisdiction over alleged crimes committed there between 2011 and 2019.
snippet: The Philippines left the ICC in 2019, on Duterte's instructions, but the court has ruled that it still has jurisdiction over alleged crimes committed there between 2011 and 2019.

title: Zelenskyy sent letter asking Trump for Patriot air defence munitions, adviser confirms
